tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: From the Philippines to Glory: Alexandra Eala Topples World No. 3 at Wimbledon
stories.summary: Twenty-one-year-old Filipino tennis star Alexandra Eala defeats world No. 3 Iga Swiatek at Wimbledon, making historic Philippine tennis history.

stories.headline: Two Lives Restored: Stem Cell 'Reset' Frees Patients From Devastating Autoimmune Disease
stories.summary: Two 28-year-old patients with neuromyelitis optica remain relapse-free for over 15 years after stem cell transplant, with one now fathering children.

stories.headline: School Officer Rents Entire Theater, Treats 144 Low-Income Students to Movie Magic
stories.summary: School officer Sean Reavie rented an entire theater and bought snacks for 144 middle schoolers to watch Toy Story 5 together.

stories.headline: From Near Death to Yellow Jersey: Vingegaard's Redemption at Tour de France
stories.summary: Jonas Vingegaard, who nearly died in a 2024 crash, returns to wear the yellow jersey at Tour de France after almost three years away.

stories.headline: Stranger Returns Late Father's Treasured Hat, Reunites Heartbroken Sisters
stories.summary: A stranger who bought Rachel White's late father's Australian hat at a charity shop for £10 saw a Facebook appeal and returned it.

stories.headline: Kids' Fight Against Unfair Permit Fees Inspires Michigan to Free Lemonade Stands
stories.summary: The Mielke brothers—Ethan, 13, Seth, 11, and Jonathan, 8—inspired Michigan lawmakers to unanimously pass a bill freeing lemonade stands from $400 permit fees.

stories.headline: First Assamese Woman Conquers English Channel Relay in Epic Endurance Feat
stories.summary: Ankita Konwar from Assam becomes the first Assamese woman to complete the English Channel relay swim in 13 hours and 5 minutes.

stories.headline: British Wildcard Arthur Fery Battles Back from Brink to Shock Wimbledon Favorites
stories.summary: British wildcard Arthur Fery battles back from two breaks down to reach Wimbledon's fourth round, beating Zizou Bergs in four hours and 38 minutes.

stories.headline: At 92, Sweet Alice Harris Continues Five Decades of Grassroots Community Care
stories.summary: At 92, beloved activist Sweet Alice Harris distributed food, clothing, and essentials to Watts families, continuing her lifelong mission of community service.

stories.headline: World's Rarest Pigs Released in India: Pygmy Hog Saved From Extinction
stories.summary: Jersey's Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust released 15 pygmy hogs—the world's smallest and rarest pigs—in India, part of a 194-hog breeding recovery.

stories.headline: Minimally Invasive Procedure Cuts Knee Pain in Half, Restoring Movement and Joy
stories.summary: A breakthrough procedure using gel beads cuts knee pain from 7 to 3 out of 10, letting seniors move freely again without major surgery.

stories.headline: Nature's Quiet Miracle: Abandoned Farmland Blooms Into Species-Rich Wildflower Meadow
stories.summary: Abandoned English farmland naturally bloomed into a species-rich wildflower meadow within 15 years through simple annual hay cutting alone.

stories.headline: Painless Nose Spray Vaccine Helps TB Patients Heal Faster and Stay Healed
stories.summary: Johns Hopkins researchers created a painless nasal DNA vaccine that helps TB patients clear the disease faster and prevents relapse after antibiotics.
